text.heading: Fed Govt urged to support Lekki Free Trade Zone
text.p: The Federal Govern-ment has been called upon to support the ongoing development of the Lekki Free Trade Zone (LFTZ) by providing not just the regulatory frame work but to support the project financially.
LFTZ is an initiative of the Lagos State government in partnership with the Chinese Consortium Company (CCC).
The Chairman of Nigeria Export Processing Zones Authority (NEPZA), Mallam Abbah Dabo, made this call on Wednesday in Lagos after inspecting the development at the site.

Describing the project as a magnificent idea that would help fast track economic development as well as create employment opportunities for millions of Nigerians, Dabo said: "I am amazed at this incredible thought."
He said this could only be possible because Lagos State is endowed with resources as well as a focused leader committed to facing and addressing challenges in the society.
According to him, "This idea is not new, it has worked in other society like Japan, Germany and others and it would certainly be a huge success in Lagos."

Explaining that no economy can survive without the vital support of investors, the chairman, however, stressed the need for the government to put in place facilities that would enable business thrive.
He identified the need to make provision for stable and effective power supply, which he said was one major factor investors in the country were finding very challenging.
He also emphasised the need to establish a training institute in the zone where workers would undergo periodic training.
He said this would help open up and diversify the revenue base of the economy as well as promotion of export trade.

He said with a huge population and huge market, the country has all it takes to emerge as the economic giant, adding that all that it requires is the necessary support from the government as well as the provision of the infrastructure that would enable businesses thrive.
"The state government has done quite well and I think it would be necessary for the Federal Government to be involved in this laudable project."
He also canvassed for support from the private sector.

Earlier, the special Adviser to Governor Babatunde Fashola, Mrs. Shola Oworu, who conducted the members of NEPZA board round the facility, said that about 68 investors had indicated interest in the project, while 12 had made their deposit payment. The state government, he said, is in so much hurry to develop the zone.

Mrs Oworu said an alternative power supply had been provided for the investors while the government was working on an Independent Power Supply as a permanent solution to the problem of constant electricity for the zone.
She said among the industries that had indicated interest include the oil and gas, textile as well as the manufacturing companies.
Mrs Oworu said the zone was open to all sector while explaining that the idea was to focus on skill transfer as well create employment opportunities.

She said these first set of investors were Nigerians who believed in what the government is doing and are ready to ensure that the project does not only become a reality but an economic reference point for the nation.
